name: Riópar from Elche de la Sierra — circular by the Sierra del Agua
name_original: Riópar desde Elche de la Sierra — circular por la Sierra del Agua
region_intro: Road cycling around Elche De La Sierra is characterized by a mountainous environment within the Sierra del Segura region. The landscape features numerous hills, valleys, and significant elevation changes, with the Segura River forming a prominent natural boundary. Cyclists can expect routes with mostly well-paved surfaces, offering diverse terrain from moderate to very challenging. This area provides a varied backdrop for road cycling, including river canyons, reservoirs, and numerous viewpoints. Best road cycling routes around Elche De La Sierra The most popular road cycling route is Riópar from Elche de la Sierra — circular by the Sierra del Agua, a 73.7 miles (118.5 km) trail that takes 6 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route passes through the Sierra del Agua, offering substantial elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Aýna y Liétor — circular desde Elche de la Sierra, a difficult 40.1 miles (64.5 km) path. This route features a significant climb and traverses scenic areas including the towns of Aýna and Liétor. Local road cyclists also love the Letur and the Embalse de la Fuensanta — circular desde Elche de la Sierra, a 49.6 miles (79.8 km) trail leading through a landscape with a 1,350-meter climb, often completed in about 4 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Elche De La Sierra is defined by mountainous terrain, the Segura River, and numerous viewpoints. The network offers options for moderate to very challenging ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Elche De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Elche de la Sierra?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Elche de la Sierra, offering a variety of experiences. Most of these, about 18, are classified as difficult, with a few moderate options for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Elche de la Sierra?
A: The terrain around Elche de la Sierra is predominantly mountainous, situated within the Sierra del Segura region. Cyclists can expect numerous hills, valleys, and significant elevation changes. The routes generally feature mostly well-paved surfaces, providing a challenging yet rewarding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Elche de la Sierra?
A: While specific weather patterns aren't detailed, the region's mountainous environment suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder conditions in winter. Always check local forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there options for less experienced road cyclists?
A: While the majority of routes are classified as difficult, there are approximately 6 moderate road cycling routes available around Elche de la Sierra. These routes still feature elevation changes but are designed to be less strenuous than the more challenging options.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders can find long-distance challenges. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164878486/riopar-from-elche-de-la-sierra-circular-by-the-sierra-del-agua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar from Elche de la Sierra — circular by the Sierra del Agua&lt;/a&gt; route spans nearly 119 km with over 2,180 meters of elevation gain, making it a substantial ride.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164899738/ayna-y-lietor-circular-desde-elche-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aýna y Liétor — circular desde Elche de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164939961/letur-and-the-embalse-de-la-fuensanta-circular-desde-elche-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Letur and the Embalse de la Fuensanta — circular desde Elche de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cyclists can enjoy diverse natural features, including the Segura River, river canyons, and the serene Cenajo Reservoir. The landscape is also dotted with conifer forests, low mountain vegetation, and agricultural areas like cereal crops and vineyards.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth visiting on a road cycling tour?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its breathtaking panoramas. Notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador La Rodea Grande&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views of Aýna, and others like Mirador del Segura y el Cenajo, Mirador del Diablo, and Mirador de las Mayas.

Q: Can I visit charming towns or historical sites while road cycling?
A: Yes, several routes pass through or near charming towns and historical sites. You can explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5596072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aýna&lt;/a&gt;, a picturesque town, or visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2745217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Letur&lt;/a&gt;, a Historic-Artistic Site with a well-preserved medieval layout. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6006591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bogarra&lt;/a&gt; also offers narrow, steep streets and river views.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural pools accessible from the cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can find refreshing natural pools and waterfalls. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2745212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de Las Canales&lt;/a&gt; is a natural pool with crystalline waters, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5639374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Natural Pools of Arroyo Rambla de Benízar&lt;/a&gt; offer beautiful spots for a break.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Elche de la Sierra?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, you can typically find parking within the town of Elche de la Sierra itself, or in the smaller towns and villages that serve as starting points for many of the circular routes, such as Aýna or Letur.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the towns and villages along or near the cycling routes, such as Elche de la Sierra, Aýna, Letur, and Bogarra, offer various options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation. These can provide convenient stops for refreshments or overnight stays during longer tours.

Q: Are the road cycling routes suitable for families?
A: Given that most road cycling routes in Elche de la Sierra are classified as difficult due to significant elevation changes, they may not be ideal for families with young children or those seeking very easy, flat rides. However, families with older, experienced cyclists might enjoy the moderate options available.
tips: A balcony-viewpoint located at a higher level than the road (you have to go up a staircase), overlooking the valley on a large rock protected by a stone wall and a metal fence. It has an information panel. Spectacular views of Aýna and the mountains.

The viewpoint also called "Sidecar" for the replica of the scooter from the film "Amanece que no es poco" by José Luis Cuerda. The film was shot between the towns of Aýna, Molinicos and Letur in 1988. The viewpoint offers wonderful views of the rocks on which the town of Aýna is located. Very recommendable.

An incredible viewpoint overlooking the valley and the Mundo River. Very wild and wooded. It has an information panel. It has a small parking lot. Everything around is truly amazing. Don't hesitate to stop.

Elche de la Sierra, although it is a small municipality (it has 3,500 inhabitants) is well known for the “Alfombras de Serrín” party that is celebrated the night before Corpus Christi, making carpets out of sawdust and shavings, obtaining incredible mosaics or portraits of religious figures. . The festival attracts many tourists and was declared in 2014 of National Tourist Interest.

waypoints.name: View of Elche de la Sierra
